title: arum italicum subsp. majoricense
intro: Arum italicum subsp. majoricense is a subspecies of the Arum italicum plant, commonly known as Italian arum, a perennial plant belonging to the Arum family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Arum italicum subsp. majoricense include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and cholesterol. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and powdering the roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
